Aisha gets a solid 7.5 hours of restorative sleep after a full day off; the sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Monday turned out to be exactly what I needed. Woke up early, grabbed coffee at home, then drove downtown to Pike Place Market—the sun was perfect, not too hot yet. Grabbed some fresh strawberries and a croissant, then met up with a few people at Matt's for brunch. We ended up watching the USA-Wales match at the pub next door, which was way more fun than I expected. Everyone was so into it, and honestly, I just needed to be around people and not think about the ER for a few hours. Walked through Capitol Hill after, checked out some new boutiques, then headed home. Spent the rest of the afternoon doing the domestic thing—grocery run, meal prep, a long bath. Started this true crime series I've been meaning to watch. Now I'm just sitting here on the couch, full from dinner, feeling the kind of tired that's actually peaceful.
thought_process: I think I needed this Monday more than I realized. Work's been grinding me down lately—the ER doesn't really slow down, and I've been running on fumes. When I saw the weather was gorgeous and the World Cup match was on, I just decided to treat myself to a full day off the usual routine. The Light Rail closure didn't even matter because I drove. I lingered at Pike Place, let myself enjoy brunch without rushing, and the match was the perfect excuse to be social without any pressure. By afternoon, I was ready to come home and just... exist. The bath and true crime combo is my reset button. I'm going into the week feeling more human than I have in weeks.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
